promptHead:
You are part of an ADVISORY design review of the proposed hmpsn.studio platform rebuild (undefined). No code changes ever — findings only.
Repo: /Users/joshuahampson/CascadeProjects/asset-dashboard. The UI Rebuild Kit (the proposed new platform) lives at "/Users/joshuahampson/CascadeProjects/asset-dashboard/hmpsn studio Design System" — the hi-fi prototype is mockup/*.js view modules; HTML docs ex

structuredOutput.topic: Business fit: is the proposed UI Rebuild Kit the right build for hmpsn.studio (solo-founder SEO agency, tiered monetization, Issue-led retention)?

keyFindings.finding: Client-side monetization machinery (TierGate, 14-day trial, Plans/ROI, cart-checkout, per-item content purchase, upgrade moments) has no designed home anywhere in the kit — absent from the 18-surface map, the Platform Parity Ledger, the client mockup, and the Content & Access permissions matrix, despite being the business's revenue model.
keyFindings.evidence: Platform Parity Ledger.html: zero matches for cart/checkout/purchase/'Client portal'/Plans/trial (only admin-side Stripe rows: Settings config, Revenue dashboard, pipeline.js:476-539 subscription plans). Client Dashboard Mockup.html: only monetization artifact is a static 'Growth plan' badge. Content & Access Conventions: role-only matrix (Operator/Admin/Client), no tier axis, one locked-microcopy example. MONETIZATION.md defines the 3-tier + per-item + trial model; FEATURE_AUDIT.md #522 shows tier gating is live and server-authoritative (computeEffectiveTier).

keyFindings.finding: The client mockup renders Premium-tier service semantics under a Growth badge — 'Your turn: 2 approvals', 'Curated by your strategist', 'Ask your strategist' shown for a 'Growth plan' client — contradicting MONETIZATION.md where Growth is explicitly zero-human-touch (no approvals, no requests) and approvals/strategist touch are the $999 Premium proposition. Built as-is it over-promises solo-founder labor at $249/mo or silently redefines the tiers.
keyFindings.evidence: Client Dashboard Mockup.html header ('Bayview Dental … Growth plan', 'Ask your strategist') and renderOverview() strings ('Your turn', '2 approvals', 'Curated by your strategist'); MONETIZATION.md Growth tier: 'pure SaaS margin with zero human touch… No requests, no approvals, no team hours'; 'SEO change approvals' listed under Premium.

keyFindings.finding: Three conflicting client IAs coexist in the kit: the shipped, owner-ratified Issue four-tab shell (Overview·Inbox·Results·Deep Dive) which the kit's own Client Surface Sweep calls 'the current, correct design — the current one already won, finish the cutover, don't redesign'; the Client Dashboard Plan's Overview·Performance·Strategy·Inbox; and the Mockup's five tabs adding Brand. A fresh third IA risks repeating the exact build-beside-never-retire disease the Sweep diagnoses (CL1–CL5) and relitigating a ratified decision.
keyFindings.evidence: Client Surface Sweep.html §01/§05 ('The current design is the right one… finish the switch to the one that already won'); Client Dashboard Plan.html §03 (four tabs); Client Dashboard Mockup.html placeholder() map (performance/deepdive/inbox/brand); Handoff Brief Phase 0 gate acknowledges the decision is open.

keyFindings.finding: The client-dashboard decision would be signed off on one designed screen: only Overview is real in the mockup; Performance, Deep Dive, Inbox, and Brand are placeholder stubs — yet the retention thesis's non-negotiable decision queue, the trust guards around approvals/reviews, and most pay-more moments live in those undesigned tabs.
keyFindings.evidence: Client Dashboard Mockup.html placeholder(k) function returns stub cards ('Building this next — the Overview sets the pattern'); Issue spec §G non-negotiable #3 (what-needs-ME queue) and §D expansion levers map to Inbox/Strategy/Performance surfaces.

keyFindings.finding: Scope truth the plan must name: retention lives off-dashboard. The Issue spec mandates 'do not design for habitual logins' — the return hooks are event-driven push and the forwardable export. The kit covers the export (shareable PDF in the mockup) but no notification/email design, and the pending roadmap items in that loop (email preference center + CAN-SPAM, named-record reconciliation — the #1 trust guard) are untouched. The rebuild polishes the room clients visit once a month.
keyFindings.evidence: Issue spec §F ('the dashboard is where you land from a notification, not a destination'); data/roadmap.json 'The Issue (Client) — Verdict-First Trust Spine' pending items; mockup string 'Generating a shareable PDF of your results'.

keyFindings.finding: Free-tier and trial client experiences are undesigned — the mockup assumes a fully-populated Growth workspace; the funnel top (Free) and the conversion window (trial) have no client view, despite the admin side getting a designed cold-start ('Cold start = onboarding, not empty').
keyFindings.evidence: Surface Model.html cold-start section is admin-cockpit only; Client Dashboard Plan A5 names the free-tier empty-tab problem but the mockup does not address it; MONETIZATION.md free-tier strategy.

keyFindings.finding: Opportunity-cost sequencing: the rebuild is by mandate 're-presentation, not reduction' (zero new capability) across 18 surfaces while revenue features pend — Strategy v3 paid-topic monetization spine (deferred), the entire Self-Service & Growth sprint (5/5 pending), Premium hours tracking, post-subscription welcome moment.
keyFindings.evidence: UI Rebuild Handoff Brief Phase 0 mandate; data/roadmap.json pending sprints 'Self-Service & Growth', 'Client Dashboard Followups', Strategy v3 deferred monetization spine.

keyFindings.finding: The admin IA is a genuine solo-operator design, not ceremony: Today home replaces 9 stat cards + four triage systems with one cross-client work queue including an explicit 'monetization plays' stream and provenance-aware money; the scope×audience grid + send boundary + graduation path matches the shipped Issue curation workflow; Keywords 2→1 and Site Audit 3→1 reduce operator surface area.
keyFindings.evidence: mockup/home.js header comment ('WHAT SHOULD I WORK ON TODAY?… What monetization plays can grow revenue'; provenance chips estimate→measured→actual); Surface Model.html grid and graduation path.

keyFindings.finding: The client Overview mockup is a faithful build of the Issue spec spine — dollarized verdict vs retainer with dated baseline, human-unit outcome counts, 'Your turn' queue leading, outcome-tagged shipped work, estimate labeling (~/≈), forwardable PDF — and the 18-surface map aligns with the pending roadmap (Local Presence, AI Visibility, Recommendations, Competitors, Outcomes surfaces all exist).
keyFindings.evidence: Mockup code comment '══ OVERVIEW = THE ISSUE ══'; strings '~$18,400 /mo · 4.6× your retainer', '41 calls · 12 form fills · 6 bookings', 'up from 21 since we started'; mockup/local*.js, aivis.js, recs.js, outcomes.js vs data/roadmap.json pending sprints.

recommendations: Design the tier layer before any client surface is built: add a tier×trial axis to the Content & Access permissions matrix and to Handoff Brief template field 5; add parity-ledger rows for TierGate/trial/Plans-ROI/cart-checkout/per-item purchase; design the 'upgrade at the ceiling' moment and the included/priced/locked act-on states as design-system components.
Resolve the Growth-badge vs strategist-touch/approvals contradiction explicitly — fix the mockup's tier semantics or deliberately revise MONETIZATION.md; never hand the ambiguity to a build agent.

Make the Phase 0 client-dashboard decision default to the kit's own Client Surface Sweep recommendation — finish the ratified Issue IA cutover and evolve it with the mockup's additive ideas (hero verdict band, Request-this move cards, Brand trust panel); a net-new parallel shell must prove it won't recreate CL1–CL5.
Do not close the client-dashboard gate on Overview alone — require Inbox (action loop + trust guards) and at least one designed pay-more moment first.

Name the retention scope boundary in the plan: the rebuild is the landing surface; event-driven push, the email preference center, and named-record reconciliation are the actual return/trust loop and stay prioritized independently.
Design the Free-tier and 14-day-trial client states (soft-gated view, trial moment, post-subscription welcome) as part of the client proposal, not after.

Sequence the surface fan-out revenue-first (Client portal, Recommendations, Pipeline early) so monetization-adjacent surfaces land before long-tail admin tools and the rebuild never freezes revenue work.

promptHead:
Stay fully in character. Answer ONLY from this persona's perspective and self-interest. Advisory review — no files written, no git.
PERSONA: Multi-location/franchise operator (15-40 clinics, one P&L, one agency relationship). Ops-bred; buys triage and a decision, not SEO.
DISTRUST TRIGGERS: Per-location spelunking; no portfolio triage; averages hiding outliers.

YOUR OWN PRIOR SPEC: in June you an

Stay fully in character. Answer ONLY from this persona's perspective and self-interest. Advisory review — no files written, no git.
PERSONA: Marketing lead at a ~40-partner professional-services consulting firm. Non-technical; sits between the agency and managing partners; judges everything by whether it defends the retainer in front of partners.
DISTRUST TRIGGERS: Anything I cannot forward upward

structuredOutput.persona: Marketing lead at a ~40-partner professional-services consulting firm. Non-technical; sits between the agency and the managing partners; judges everything by whether it defends the retainer in front of partners who'd rather fund a conference booth.
resolved: The spine inversion I demanded is now the whole platform's religion, not a patch: VERDICT → VALUE → PROOF opens every client surface, and the raw SEO cockpit (impressions, rank tables, health gauge) is demoted to a collapsed 'Under the hood' drawer. I can open this for ninety seconds before a partner meeting and lead with a conclusion.

My homework is first-class and up top: the 'Your turn' strip, '1 guide awaiting your OK', and the conversation thread with Approve/Request-a-change make the dashboard my to-do list with the agency — the only return hook that reliably works on me.

The forwardable one-pager finally EXISTS: 'Share this as a one-pager — a clean PDF of your results, forward it to a partner or your accountant' (Client Dashboard Mockup). That is verbatim the single feature I said would make me log in every month on purpose.

Named leads with a privacy guard ('Sarah K. — called from the emergency-dentist page' · 'Only you can see this') — the shape of my trust linchpin (actual inquiries I can point a partner at) is designed, plus the honest only-when-provable variant ('lead tracking connects once your form is wired up') instead of a fabricated number.

Provenance honesty is systemic: estimate/measured/actual chips, banded dollars, 'agency estimate' badges on every client-facing hero, methodology disclosure ('not a promise of future revenue'), and 'exact once reconciled against your booked revenue.' This is the anti-spin posture I said buys trust.

Credibility protection is substantially wired into the content loop: topic approval before writing, briefs and drafts gated on client sign-off ('awaiting client'), and an AI review where factual-accuracy and hallucination gates NEVER auto-pass — a human always checks before anything ships under our name. That's most of my 'I'd churn over one embarrassing partner-bylined piece' guard.

The work log is outcome-tagged in language I can forward: 'What we did this month' rows with a plain-English what/why and a value, not 'fixed 14 broken links.' And my requests become strategy signals ('Promote to signal'), so what I ask for visibly shapes the plan.
stillMissing: My #1 credibility ask, again: the topic-vs-named-competitor authority slot — 'are we page-one for the 15-20 topics partners get hired for, vs the two firms we lose deals to.' The portal shows generic 'page-one terms'; the dashboard's competitor block is 'they have 3× your Google reviews' buried under the hood; the real Competitors view is admin-side. Third review in a row where the screen my managing partner actually Googles doesn't exist client-facing.

Qualified-inbound QUALITY: no title/firm/topic tagging and no junk filter. 'Enquiries from search' is still a raw count — students, vendors, and competitor-snoops inflate it exactly as I warned. Named leads are a fixture gesture, not a designed 'right kind of prospect' contract.

Event-driven nudges — the entire return-hook layer from my spec ('new qualified inquiry from [topic]', 'competitor overtook you on a deal-driving term', 'partner piece waiting for your 5-min review' pushed to email/Slack) is absent. The portal's hook is 'since your last visit', which still requires me to remember to visit.

Segment adaptivity is nowhere: every fixture is a dentist or a kitchen retailer, and the money arithmetic is visits × 2.1% CVR × $705 avg order — e-commerce math. My firm sells seven-figure engagements on 9-month cycles; my segment's pipeline-altitude money frame (promised in the P0 review as P1) hasn't materialized in this redesign at all.

Brand/reputation-mention monitoring (being cited in the outlets that get us hired) — not present anywhere. AI-answer visibility exists but as an admin workbench only; nothing in MY portal tells me whether ChatGPT names us or Competitor X for our expertise areas — the thing I said I'd pay a premium for.

No period anchor: the portal is one scrolling page with no monthly framing, no history, and — critically — NO export; the one-pager lives only in the OTHER client design. My cadence is the monthly partner update; 'since your last visit' doesn't map to it.
distrustTriggers: The portal's flagship verdict fixture is 'You're climbing — traffic's up 34% this quarter.' A TRAFFIC-led verdict is the exact vanity-dressed-as-a-win I said would start me interviewing replacements. The digest says copy is placeholder, but the verdict template itself hardcodes trajectory = sessions delta — the rule, not just the copy, leads with traffic instead of inquiries.

TWO competing client-facing designs ship in the same kit with the decision explicitly unresolved. My indispensable features are split between them: the one-pager export and named leads live only in the tabbed dashboard (4 of 5 tabs placeholder); the approve/reply loop and story spine live in the portal. Whichever one loses the gate, I lose something I was promised — and 'we haven't decided what your portal is' is not a sentence I can take to partners.

'$18,400/mo · 4.6× your retainer' as the headline, with '≈ 9,600 missed visits × 2.1% × $705 avg order' spelled out beneath it. My managing partners would mock '$705 average order' in a consulting context within seconds, and the first mocked number poisons every other number on the page. Aggressive dollarization with e-commerce arithmetic is a jargon wall in dollar clothing — I cannot forward this upward unedited.

One-tap 'Yes — write this' commissions content straight into the pipeline from the portal. For a firm where content ships under a partner's byline, a one-tap greenlight with no named reviewer or byline step visible at the moment of commitment makes me nervous — the dashboard's 'your strategist scopes and confirms first' framing is the right one; the portal's isn't.

The kit's own internal inconsistencies (Site Audit '3→1' while Performance and Links still ship separately; a declared-but-empty book zone; purple tinting a nav group against its own color law). None of it is client-facing, but if the agency's spec contradicts itself, I discount the promises made in it — including the ones made to me.
wouldImprove: Resolve the two-design decision NOW, and whichever shell wins, contractually carry over my three non-negotiables: the zero-edit one-pager export, named/clickable inbound records, and the approve-before-ship loop. Don't make me discover post-launch that my export died with the losing prototype.

Change the verdict RULE, not the fixture: the client verdict must lead with the outcome noun (inquiries/leads), never a traffic percentage. Traffic may appear as supporting proof, never as the headline sentence. Write that into the handoff brief as a hard law like the color laws.

Build the authority slot for my segment: my 15-20 expertise topics vs 2-3 NAMED competitor firms, page-one yes/no plus movement ('you passed Competitor X on [topic]'). It's a table with maybe 40 rows — the data exists in your Competitors and Keywords surfaces; it's a projection, not a new engine. This is the third review where I've asked.

Make the money frame segment-aware: for professional services, kill visits × CVR × AOV and show 'SEO-influenced inquiries → estimated pipeline' at deal-band altitude, reconciled against the named-inquiry list. An honest smaller number I can defend beats a big number partners can mock.

Turn the one-pager from a button into a monthly push: auto-generate it on my reporting cadence and email it to me with a two-line summary. The export I must remember to click is the export I forget; the one that arrives the week of my partner update is the reason I renew.

Tag inbound with title/firm/topic and split 'right-kind' from junk explicitly (even manually by the strategist at first). And put the partner-byline review into my 'Your turn' strip by name: 'Partner piece under [Partner]'s byline awaiting your 5-minute review' — fuse the credibility gate into my decision loop as my spec asked.

Ship a client-visible AI-answers line ('when a prospect asks ChatGPT who to hire for [topic], you are named / Competitor X is named') graduated from the AI Visibility workbench — you already built the engine; give me the one sentence from it I can repeat to partners, because nobody else is selling me that.
structuredOutput.verbatim: You finally built the honest, decision-first briefing I asked for and even the forwardable one-pager I said would keep me renewing — but until you kill the traffic-led verdict, translate the $705-average-order math into pipeline language, and show me us-versus-the-named-firms-we-lose-deals-to, I still can't walk into the partner meeting and win the budget argument with it, and that's the only job this dashboard has.

promptHead:
You are part of an ADVISORY design review of the proposed hmpsn.studio platform rebuild (undefined). No code changes ever — findings only.
Repo: /Users/joshuahampson/CascadeProjects/asset-dashboard. The UI Rebuild Kit (the proposed new platform) lives at "/Users/joshuahampson/CascadeProjects/asset-dashboard/hmpsn studio Design System" — the hi-fi prototype is mockup/*.js view modules; HTML docs ex

structuredOutput.topic: Visual design & accessibility soundness of the hmpsn studio UI Rebuild Kit

keyFindings.finding: The mandated 'tabular numerals on all data' is physically impossible with the shipped font: D-DIN-PRO OTFs have no tnum OpenType feature and proportional digit widths, so every font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums on DIN-set values (MetricTile, GroupBlock, Meter, mockup .num class) is a silent no-op — rank/volume columns will misalign and live stats will jitter.
keyFindings.evidence: Parsed assets/fonts/D-DIN-PRO-700-Bold.otf: GSUB features = dlig/frac/liga/ordn/sups only; digit advances '1'=348 vs '8'=525 units (Regular '1'=329 vs '8'=512). Handoff Brief house rule 6 mandates tabular numerals; MetricTile.jsx:31, GroupBlock.jsx:43, Meter.jsx:16 request tnum on var(--font-display). DataTable/KeyValueRow work only because they inherit Inter (which has tnum).

keyFindings.finding: Purple reaches client-facing surfaces through the spec itself, violating the fourth law ('purple = admin-AI only, never client-facing'): the client portal mockup uses purple for next-step dots/pills/rec icons, IntentTag maps the 'local' intent to purple, and the new Badge reintroduces the purple tone the current platform deliberately removed from its Badge color union.
keyFindings.evidence: mockup/portal.js lines 23, 155, 158, 201-207 (hard-coded #7c3aed in the client portal); components/feedback/IntentTag.jsx:15 (local → purple); components/feedback/Badge.jsx:11 (purple tone) vs root CLAUDE.md Four Laws §4 ('Purple removed from Badge.tsx color union').

keyFindings.finding: The flagship interaction (table row → detail drawer) is keyboard-inaccessible in the core primitives every surface will assemble from: DataTable clickable rows are div onClick with no tabIndex/role/key handler and no table semantics; Drawer/Modal/Popover have zero focus trap, focus return, or Escape handling; and a closed Drawer's off-screen content remains in the tab order inside an aria-hidden region.
keyFindings.evidence: components/data/DataTable.jsx:35-48 (div rows, JS-only hover); components/layout/Drawer.jsx (translateX-only close, aria-hidden with focusable children); grep across Drawer/Modal/Popover: no onKeyDown/Escape/.focus( anywhere. Reference Screen - Keywords.html builds its primary flow on exactly these components.

keyFindings.finding: MetricRing's score bands contradict the platform's settled score-color law: ≥80 renders teal while platform canon is emerald, with a doc comment falsely claiming it 'Matches the app's scoreColor' — a client-facing health-score meaning change the brief forbids, and a regression of a fix the platform already made once.
keyFindings.evidence: components/data/MetricRing.jsx:4-8 (score>=80 → var(--teal)) vs src/components/ui/constants.ts:15 (#34d399 emerald) and constants.ts:54 comment 'FOUR-LAWS FIX: the ≤10 band is emerald (success), NOT teal (actions)'.

keyFindings.finding: --brand-text-dim (#71717a) fails WCAG AA on every surface it sits on (3.67:1 on surface-2, 3.08:1 on surface-3) yet is a sanctioned text tier used for small real text: 10px DataTable headers, t-micro, drawer eyebrows, 9px NavItem meta.
keyFindings.evidence: Computed contrast ratios from tokens/colors.css values; consumers at guidelines/type-scale.css:23, DataTable.jsx:28-29, Drawer.jsx eyebrow, NavItem.jsx meta.

keyFindings.finding: Light-mode text contrast fails exactly where the Handoff Brief warned: light mint #0d9488 on white is 3.74:1 (below AA for the 10-13.5px text it colors) and --brand-text-faint #94a3b8 is 2.56:1, used for real text in the client portal (Powered-by line, rank meta, timestamps) — the client trust surface has the worst contrast in the kit.
keyFindings.evidence: Computed from tokens/colors.css light scope; portal.js pt-power (10.5px), .rmeta (11px), .when using the faint value; Handoff Brief house rule 7 explicitly demands the light-mode accent recheck.

keyFindings.finding: The brief's ≥44px hit-target floor is contradicted by every shipped control except Toggle (Button lg≈42px/md≈35px/sm≈27px, IconButton 28-40px, NavItem≈31px, close buttons 30px), making the CI-gated floor either fail everything or be ignored.
keyFindings.evidence: components/buttons/Button.jsx:21-25 size paddings, IconButton.jsx:15 box sizes, NavItem.jsx padding, Drawer/Modal 30px close buttons vs Handoff Brief house rule 7 ('≥44px hit targets').

keyFindings.finding: Badge/Toast accent tints are frozen dark-mode rgba literals that don't re-key under .dashboard-light, breaking 'theme-neutral by construction' inside the most-used primitive; and overlay components ignore the z-index token scale with raw values (Popover 59/60, Tooltip 70, Drawer 90/95, Modal 100) so a Toast at the documented --z-toast:60 renders beneath open drawers/modals, hiding mutation feedback.
keyFindings.evidence: components/feedback/Badge.jsx:3-13 and Toast.jsx:3-8 (hard-coded rgba + '#0a1f1b'); tokens/effects.css --z-modal:50/--z-toast:60 vs raw zIndex values in Popover.jsx:15-19, Tooltip.jsx:29, Drawer.jsx (90/95), Modal.jsx (100).

keyFindings.finding: No responsive contract exists: AppShell is a fixed 232px+1fr grid at all viewports with no collapse breakpoint, layout.css breakpoints are unconsumed 'reference values', and nothing states which surfaces are mobile-required — despite the client portal being the surface clients will open from email on phones.
keyFindings.evidence: components/layout/AppShell.jsx (fixed gridTemplateColumns, manual rail prop only); tokens/layout.css:28-31; mockup shell overflow:hidden with zero @media; only portal.js and AppShell declare container-type.

keyFindings.finding: Internal law drift in semantic components will be cloned 18-wide: StatusBadge maps live→teal while the readme lists 'Live' pills under emerald; Meter (a data/progress display) defaults to mint/mint→emerald against the 'blue = progress bars' law and the Reference Screen renders the Opportunity data column as mint meters; cockpit's flight meter hard-codes purple #a78bfa as a pipeline-stage color.
keyFindings.evidence: components/feedback/StatusBadge.jsx:12; components/data/Meter.jsx defaults; Reference Screen - Keywords.html:80 (Meter gradient on Opportunity column); mockup/cockpit.js:213 (raw hex stage dots incl. #a78bfa); readme.md four-laws section.

keyFindings.finding: The Reference Screen — the declared structural target — violates the kit's own icon rule (hand-rolled Lucide paths, no FA kit loaded; Toast docs also recommend Lucide) and hand-rolls the eyebrow micro-label inline 3×, the exact defect the readme says to flag in review; the mockup also ships 'retired' Fira Code in 35 of 40 views.
keyFindings.evidence: Reference Screen - Keywords.html:28-40 (Lucide glyphs) and three inline uppercase-mono section labels; components/feedback/Toast.jsx doc comment; tokens/fonts.css ('Fira Code is retired') vs grep counts (editor.js 34 uses, pipeline.js 19, mockup shell <link>s Fira Code).

keyFindings.finding: The token architecture and theming discipline are genuinely strong and worth protecting: the light theme is engineered (darkened accents computing 5.0-5.5:1 on white, inverted zinc scale), a global keyboard-only mint focus ring plus prefers-reduced-motion kill switch ships in base.css, Toggle/Checkbox use native-button semantics (Toggle with a documented 44px target), TrendBadge triple-encodes direction (sign+arrow+color), primary buttons hit 9.2:1 contrast via dark ink on mint, and the density posture (1180px dense column, 13.5px rows, verdict-first cockpit) is operator-grade rather than whitespace-over-utility.
keyFindings.evidence: tokens/colors.css light scope (computed ratios: amber 5.02, blue 5.17, emerald 5.48 on white); tokens/base.css:30-58; forms/Toggle.jsx (minHeight 44, role=switch); data/TrendBadge.jsx; --button-primary-text #0a1f1b on #2dd4bf = 9.21:1; tokens/layout.css + Reference Screen table structure.

recommendations: Before the pilot surface, resolve the numeral strategy (license a DIN cut with tnum, or mandate Inter/JetBrains Mono for all data columns and reserve DIN for headings/hero stats), and add an adherence-lint rule forbidding font-display + tabular-nums co-occurrence.

Purge client-facing purple at the source: recolor portal.js, re-map IntentTag 'local' to a non-law hue, remove purple from Badge's public tone union, and add a lint gate mirroring the platform's existing client-path purple grep.

Fix keyboard accessibility once in the four core primitives (DataTable keyboard rows + table semantics; shared focus-trap/Escape/inert utility for Drawer/Modal/Popover) and gate the pilot on a keyboard-only walkthrough of the Reference Screen.
Apply the one-line law fixes and document them in the Which-Primitive guide: MetricRing ≥80 → emerald, StatusBadge live → emerald, Meter default fill → blue, no purple pipeline-stage dots.

Run a token pass: brighten or reclassify --brand-text-dim, make light-mode mint text-safe (#0f766e for text), replace frozen rgba tints with color-mix on tokens, and re-point all overlay z-indexes at the --z-* scale so toasts sit above modals.

Write the two missing contracts: a hit-target policy (e.g. 24px desktop / 44px touch+client, stated explicitly) and a responsive contract (shell collapse behavior below --bp-md; portal and recommendations declared mobile-required).

Clean the reference artifacts before fan-out: FA icons + .eyebrow utility in the Reference Screen, Fira Code on the lint blacklist, and publish a mockup→sanctioned-type-scale remap table so agents don't guess.
Declare the missing data-viz contracts: one canonical score-band function, one categorical multi-series color ramp, and a rule that sparklines never appear without an adjacent numeric delta.

structuredOutput.topic: Flows, interactions & state design soundness (UI Rebuild Kit prototype review)

keyFindings.finding: The product's central ritual — sending the client update — has no designed flow anywhere: 'Send update to {first}' is the dead primary topbar button on 6+ views, 'Edit POV' is dead, and there is no composer, preview, confirm, sent-state, or history.
keyFindings.evidence: mockup/app.js:5-51 (no onclick on any 'Send update' primary), mockup/strategy.js:403 (Edit POV dead) and :408 ('edited by you before send' claim)

keyFindings.finding: The flagship keyword-decision flow is a navigation stub: 'Stage into Insights Engine' from the keyword drawer stages nothing — window.stageIntoIssue() only calls gotoView('issue'), carrying no state and giving no feedback.
keyFindings.evidence: mockup/keywords.js:408 → mockup/strategy.js:475 (function(){ window.gotoView('issue'); })

keyFindings.finding: Client-originated intake requests can be dismissed silently — no confirm, no toast, no undo, no record — destroying a client's portal ask with one accidental click.
keyFindings.evidence: mockup/pipeline.js:1165-1168 (dismissIntake splices with no feedback); pipeline.js:461 (intake item is 'Dana asked in the portal')

keyFindings.finding: The prototype systematically violates its own mutation contract: zero confirm dialogs exist in any flow view — cancel plan, single/bulk publish, bulk AI fixes written to Webflow, every send-to-client, and the review queue's 'P' keyboard publish are all one-click, while Build Conventions §02 mandates pessimistic+confirm for exactly these. Undo (also mandated) is delivered exactly once (recs dismiss).
keyFindings.evidence: pipeline.js:1099 (subDelete), editor.js:1687/1694/1552 (publish, bulk publish, P-key), audit.js:649-652 (bulkAccept 'written to Webflow'), recs.js:248; grep confirms no confirm/Modal in flow views; Build Conventions.html §02

promptHead:
Stay fully in character. Answer ONLY from this persona's perspective and self-interest. Advisory review — no files written, no git.
PERSONA: Account manager the studio hires at 40 workspaces. Inherits the platform cold next year.
YOUR JOB ON THIS PLATFORM: Portfolio triage across 40 workspaces: which client is on fire, which is due, which is fine — without opening each one.
DISTRUST/FRUSTRATION TR

structuredOutput.persona: Account manager hired at 40 workspaces; inherits the platform cold and lives in book-level triage — which client is on fire, which is due, which is fine — without opening each one.
resolved: A real cross-client home finally exists: the Command Center (home.js) is ONE unified work queue across the whole book, organized by Optimizations / To send / Monetization — explicitly replacing the current platform's per-workspace 9-stat home and four separate triage systems. This is the single biggest thing the current admin (navRegistry.tsx) lacks for my job.

The workspace switcher popover carries a book roll-up header — total open requests, N at risk, N in setup — plus a health dot (on track / at risk / setting up) and open-item badge per client. That's a 5-second 'who's on fire' read I don't have today.

'From your clients' is a separate, protected stream at the top of the rail — humans waiting on me (approvals, replies, requests) never get buried under machine-generated work items. Correct instinct against alert fatigue.

Book-level Site Health is a client × issue-type matrix with batch-fix framing — systemic problems (missing alt text across the book) become one action, not 40 visits. Book-level Action Results gives me a winners-vs-laggards read and feeds a 'Send book recap'.
The verdict header tells me where to start ('Start with Acme's dead links and July update, unblock Northwind — they replied…') instead of making me derive it from stat cards.
⌘K palette jumps across all surfaces AND workspaces with persisted recents — at 40 clients keyboard-first switching is how I'll actually move.
Cold-start coherence: a new client routes to Onboarding, not a broken dashboard — so 'is this client set up yet?' is legible at the switcher, which matters when I inherit the book cold.
stillMissing: No concept of DUE anywhere on the operator side. I grepped every operator surface: no due dates, no overdue state, no SLA, no 'monthly update due', no renewal countdown. 'Which client is due today' — half my job — has no data model. Ironically the client-side mockup has a '3 pages — 9d pending' staleness chip; the operator gets nothing equivalent.

Nothing is designed for 40. The work queue is a flat list (11 fixture items, 4 clients) grouped by stream, not by client — at 40 workspaces that's 100+ undifferentiated rows with no per-client rollup, no severity sort, no pagination, no snooze/dismiss. The switcher is a popover list of 4 with no search or sort.

No portfolio table: a sortable 40-row grid (health, open items, days since last client update, next due deliverable, plan/retainer, last-touched) simply doesn't exist. The switcher roll-up is the closest thing and it's three numbers in a popover.
No aging on operator work items — timestamps exist on client messages ('22m ago') but work-queue rows have no 'sitting for 9 days' signal, so stale items silently rot.
The book-wide Inbox (requests.js, ~9KB, the smallest core view) is where clients wait on me, and it has no waiting-time markers, no filters, no per-client grouping.
No multi-operator model: no assignment, ownership, or handoff notes anywhere. The studio is hiring ME — the platform still assumes one operator who already knows every client's story.
The verdict narrative and per-client cockpit verdicts are hand-authored fixtures; nothing shows how 'start with Acme' gets generated or stays trustworthy at 40 clients.
Book calendar view: content Calendar is per-client only — no cross-book view of what publishes/is owed this week.
distrustTriggers: The BOOK zone rail is literally empty (nav.js: const book = [];) — Command Center, book Site Health, and Action Results are reachable only via a logo click or ⌘K. My primary workspace has no navigation presence; that tells me book-level triage was designed second.

Everything is dollarized as 'estimate' — 40 clients × 3 monetization 'Propose' rows each is pitch spam, and headline dollar figures I can't verify on day one make me numb to the queue fast. Provenance chips help, but in the fixtures nearly everything is 'e'.

Controls that look interactive but do nothing (Keywords' intent/stage filter chips, 'Auto-share rules', calendar prev/next) — once I hit two dead controls I stop trusting every filter on the platform.

Consolidation claims contradict the shipped nav (Site Audit badged 3→1 while Performance and Links remain separate destinations) — as the person inheriting this cold, an IA that lies about itself costs me weeks.

Four hardcoded demo clients everywhere: not one screen demonstrates the flat work queue, the popover switcher, or the verdict paragraph surviving 10× the data. I've been burned by 'it demoed great at 4'.
wouldImprove: Put the book in the rail: pin Command Center, Inbox (with badge), Site Health, and Action Results as permanent BOOK-zone entries above the client block — my home shouldn't hide behind a logo click.

Add a due/SLA layer to the data model: every to-send item, content-subscription deliverable, and monthly update gets a due date; the Command Center opens with a 'Due today / Overdue' band above the three streams.
Build the portfolio table: all 40 clients, one row each, sortable by health, open items, days since last client-facing send, next due deliverable, and retainer — that page IS my morning.
Group the work queue by client with per-client caps and severity ordering, plus snooze/dismiss with a reason — a triage queue I can't clear is an anxiety generator, not a tool.
Put aging chips on every operator work item and inbox row ('waiting 6d'), mirroring the client-side '9d pending' pattern — staleness must be visible on my side of the glass.
Add owner/assignment to work items now, even if it's a single-select with one name — the studio hiring me is proof the single-operator assumption is already wrong.
Seed one demo book with 40 realistic workspaces and make the Command Center, switcher, and Site Health matrix pass review AT that scale before any build fan-out.
structuredOutput.verbatim: Give me the Command Center in the rail with a 'due today' band and a sortable 40-client table behind it, and this is the first agency platform I wouldn't have to fight — right now it's a brilliant morning briefing written for someone who only has four clients and no deadlines.

keyFindings.finding: Zero error, loading, or locked states are demonstrated in any mockup view despite Build Conventions demanding four states per surface — ~54 pieces of state copy will be invented per-surface by agents, and the demos already override the kit's empty-state table.
keyFindings.evidence: All mockup views read (portal/recs/cockpit/home/outcomes/requests) render only happy-path; Content & Access Conventions §01 gives only one-line patterns

keyFindings.finding: Client 'What's next' chips use internal pipeline jargon (briefed, proposed, drafting, ambiguous 'review'); bare unlabeled '~2.4k/mo' chips on client recs; cockpit verdict says '3 things need you' above tiles summing to 6; hardcoded 'hmpsn studio'/'your SEO team' strings against the STUDIO_NAME rule; 'Insights Engine' label rides on the legacy 'issue' view id with the five most load-bearing new terms (Insights Engine, graduate, proof point, staged move, the book) missing from GLOSSARY's proposed intake.

keyFindings.finding: The client-portal voice is the best client copy in the project's history and must be protected: 'Found in Google', 'was #10 · now #4', consequence-framed fixes ('shoppers were hitting dead ends'), an accountable declining-client narrative, and an exemplary honest degraded state (em dash + 'lead tracking connects once your form is wired up').
keyFindings.evidence: portal.js:250-262, 386-396, 297 (hook/fixes/leads copy)

keyFindings.finding: The provenance ladder ('agency estimate' basis chip on hero value, 'projected' micro-tags, operator estimate→measured→actual chips) is a designed system for money-claim honesty — the correct answer to client distrust of unsourced numbers; and recs.js's dual-register authoring (whyOp vs cliWhy with live 'What Acme sees' preview) structurally forces the admin/client translation at write time.
keyFindings.evidence: portal.js:401 basis chip, home.js:46-51 prov chips, recs.js:109-138 whyOp/cliWhy pairs + preview pane

recommendations: Before any client surface is built: strip purple from the portal client card, move the 'curated story' footer out of the client-visible card, and rewrite client-triggered toasts in client vocabulary with no admin deep-links.
Fix the format contract before format.ts exists: resolve the ≥$10k rule vs $8.9k example contradiction, add a count-compaction threshold, and mark the mockups' inline formatters as non-normative.

Add the missing admin→client term map to Content & Access Conventions (proof point→win, staged move→improvement, Action Results→never shown, etc.), inheriting shared/types/client-vocabulary.ts and the locked-copy pattern rather than re-inventing.
Ratify or revert label drift explicitly: 'Share with client'→'Send to client' (or amend ui-vocabulary deliberately), decide 'portal' vs 'Client Dashboard', and unify the three approve-CTA idioms.
Demo all four states (error/loading/locked/empty) with real copy on the reference screen before fan-out, and fix the two forbidden 'Nothing here' strings.
Add source attribution to client proof sections ('data from Google Search Console / GA4') and estimate tags on derived client visitor numbers.
Complete GLOSSARY's proposed-intake for Insights Engine, graduate, proof point, staged move, backing move, and 'the book'; reconcile the kit's 'mint' vs repo 'teal' action-hue naming in one sentence.

promptHead:
Stay fully in character. Answer ONLY from this persona's perspective and self-interest. Advisory review — no files written, no git.
PERSONA: Non-marketing SMB owner-operator (commercial HVAC, ~18 employees, ~$3M). The check-signer, not the daily user. Revenue-literate, jargon-illiterate by choice, burned by a beautiful-PDF agency before.
DISTRUST TRIGGERS: A number with no baseline; activity dress

Stay fully in character. Answer ONLY from this persona's perspective and self-interest. Advisory review — no files written, no git.
PERSONA: Churned, skeptical ex-agency client on a monthly SEO retainer, burned by a previous agency that drowned "did you get me customers?" in a fog of charts. Trust is the gate.
DISTRUST TRIGGERS: Spin, self-grading, fabricated or unverifiable numbers.

YOUR OWN PRI

structuredOutput.persona: Churned, skeptical ex-agency client — SMB owner/operator on a monthly SEO retainer, burned by a previous agency that drowned "did you get me customers?" in a fog of charts. Trust is the gate; spin, self-grading, and unverifiable numbers end the relationship.
resolved: The verdict finally leads and it's in my units with my baseline: '59 new-patient actions this month, up from 21 since we started' — one sentence, real number, dated against when I started paying. That's the exact ask I wrote in June.

The money frame isn't afraid of the comparison anymore: '~$18,400/mo · 4.6× your retainer' with my monthly investment ($4,000) printed right next to the value in the ROI ledger. They put my check on the same screen as what it bought.

Named human leads — 'Sarah K. — called from the emergency dentist page,' marked 'Only you can see this.' Actual inquiries I can check against my phone log is the single strongest trust move in the whole kit. And when tracking isn't connected, it shows an honest dash + 'connects once your form is wired up' instead of faking it.

Methodology said out loud, on my screen: 'tracked conversions × your average new-patient value ($310)... banded while it's an estimate, exact once reconciled against your booked revenue' — and the estimate → measured → actual provenance chips run through the whole system. An agency volunteering the limits of its own number is what earns the rest.
'Your turn' strip at the very top with the approvals waiting on me, including a shaming '9d pending' stale chip. Tells me if the ball's in my court before anything else.
All the SEO machinery — impressions, avg position, site-health stat bar, competitor stack — collapsed under 'Under the hood' where I open it only when I want to audit them.
The forwardable one-pager export exists ('a clean PDF — forward it to a partner or your accountant'). That's my no-hostage-situation ask.
A real two-way lane: approve the plan, request a change, one-tap 'Yes — write this,' and my messages actually land on the operator's desk. Last agency, my replies went into a void.
stillMissing: Reconciliation to MY reality — still an IOU, two reviews running. The copy admits the number is a GA4-derived estimate, but there's no 'our count vs. your phone/inbox, here's why they differ' guard anywhere in the kit. This was my #1 stated churn trigger and the deferred half of my non-negotiable, again.

The clickable work log. 'Refreshed your velvet armchair page +$1,500/mo projected' — great, WHERE'S THE LINK? Not one 'what we did' row clicks through to the live page. Verifiable proof-of-work is the literal reason I fired the last agency, and it's still missing from the design itself, not just the build.

The return hook. Nothing in the kit does SMS/email push — no 'a lead came in,' no 'we caught a problem before it cost you.' By my own admission I'll open this once a month when the invoice hits; a 'since your last visit' header only works after I've already shown up.
Call tracking/recording and lead-quality scoring — the thing I said I'd pay MORE for — is still just an 'ask your strategist' affordance, not a surface.
Garbage filtering: nothing shows spam calls, tire-kickers, or existing customers being excluded from my lead count. Inflated counts are the fastest way to lose me.

A decision. The kit ships TWO client-facing designs — the portal microsite and the tabbed dashboard — with different verdicts and different honesty levels, and the one built to my spec is one finished tab out of five. Which one do I actually get?
distrustTriggers: Dollar fog replacing chart fog: nearly every line item across every surface carries an agency-authored dollar estimate ('+$1,500/mo projected,' '$14,200/mo at stake,' '≈$2,900/mo'). Twenty unverifiable estimates stacked up feels exactly like the old wall of charts, just denominated in money I can't check.

False precision on estimated data: '$18,400/mo' and '4.6×' to a decimal, when the methodology itself says it's a banded estimate. My prior build banded to '~$12,000' — this mockup un-learned that. Precision I can't verify reads as fabrication.

Self-grading: a '90-day scorecard' where the agency scores its own work 'Win rate 68%,' with Win/Strong-win badges it awards itself and no shown formula. Who decided those were wins? A number you control with no formula is an instant trust kill — I wrote that verbatim in June.
The 94/100 site-health ring sits in the portal's main proof column — the invented composite score we just spent a redesign demoting is back on the first screen of the OTHER client design.

Down months spun as strategy: the portal verdict flips to 'We're defending your rankings — here's the plan' when things decline. That's a euphemism. Show me the dip and the number; rebranding a decline as 'defending' is the exact spin that made me leave.

The portal's headline verdict is 'traffic's up 34% this quarter' — traffic is the metric I trust LEAST, because I've watched it climb while my phone stayed dead. Leading my page with it tells me you're measuring your work, not my customers.
wouldImprove: Pick ONE client design and make it the tabbed dashboard's Overview — the one with my verdict, named leads, and retainer math — and kill the portal's traffic-led hook and its 94/100 ring before either ships to a paying client.
Make every 'what we did' row a link to the live page. If you shipped it, I can click it. No link, no credit — that single change converts the work log from a claim into proof.

Band every estimated dollar (~$18k, not $18,400) and drop the one-decimal ROI multiple; keep exact figures only for 'measured/actual' provenance. You already built the ladder — make the formatting obey it.

Replace the self-scored win rate with outcome-scored: a 'win' is defined by a movement in MY numbers (calls, inquiries, booked work), the formula printed next to it — or move the scorecard to your side of the glass entirely.

Put the reconciliation guard in the design now, not P-whatever: our count and your count side by side, with a plain-English line explaining any gap, and show me the $-per-lead value you plugged in ($310) with a 'confirm or correct this' button on MY screen.

Design the notification layer — 'a lead came in from search today,' 'we caught a problem,' 'one approval waiting' by text/email — because without it this thing gets opened exactly once a month, at the moment I'm deciding whether to fire you.
When a month is down, say 'down,' with the number and the plan — never 'defending.' An honest red month is what makes me believe your green ones.
structuredOutput.verbatim: You finally lead with my customers, my baseline, and your bill on the same screen — now give me the link to click and the number that matches my phone, and stop grading your own homework, because the day your estimate drifts from my front desk with no way to check it, I'm gone the same as last time.

promptHead:
Stay fully in character. Answer ONLY from this persona's perspective and self-interest. Advisory review — no files written, no git.
PERSONA: VC backer / board member. Thinks in CAC, payback, durability — sees this dashboard maybe once a quarter as a screenshot. One question: is organic becoming our cheapest durable channel or are we funding a treadmill?
DISTRUST TRIGGERS: Invented 0-100 scores, ra

structuredOutput.persona: VC backer / board member — sees this dashboard once a quarter as a screenshot in a board deck; thinks in CAC, payback, and durability; one question: is organic our cheapest durable channel or a treadmill we're funding?
resolved: Dollars lead everywhere — the VERDICT → VALUE → PROOF trust spine is now the organizing law of every client-facing narrative, and the headline unit is a dollar figure, not a visibility score. That was my hardest cultural demand from P0 and the kit institutionalizes it.

The board one-pager export finally exists in design — the Client Dashboard Mockup has an explicit 'Share this as a one-pager (PDF)' row on the hero. That was my deferred-P1 anti-lock-in guard and my operators' half-day-a-quarter tax.

A provenance ladder (estimate → measured → actual chips, 'agency estimate' basis badges, spelled-out arithmetic like '≈ 9,600 visits × 2.1% × $705 AOV') — visible methodology on every money figure is the attribution-transparency guard I demanded, now a system-level primitive instead of one hard-coded string.
The denominator is at least on the page: the ROI ledger shows 'your monthly investment $4,000/mo' next to value — P0 only used retainer as an invisible ratio divisor.

Real reconciliation gestures: named leads ('Sarah K. — called from the emergency-dentist page'), a bonus-leads tile that renders a dash + 'connect tracking' when unproven instead of a fabricated number, and a leads breakdown with an honest 'names available with call & CRM tracking' upsell. Refusing to show the unprovable is exactly the restraint that buys my belief.

Time-over-time is back: 'since your last visit' deltas, 12-month charts, 6-month action sparklines, a verdict that flips to 'We're defending your rankings' on bad trajectory — the evergreen no-baseline stance that I flagged as recreating all-green-in-spirit has been abandoned.

Anti-feature burial mostly holds in the dashboard mockup: raw visitors/clicks/impressions, site health, and the competitor stack live in a collapsed 'Under the hood' with a 'not a promise of future revenue' disclaimer.
stillMissing: Organic CAC vs blended/paid CAC — my stated indispensable, the entire reason this line item survives budget review — appears nowhere in either client design. The ROI ledger's 'equivalent ad spend $21,300/mo' is the cost-to-buy-traffic proxy I explicitly said is not my frame; it tells me what the traffic would cost to rent, not what a customer costs to acquire.
Payback in months — absent from every surface.

The defensibility slope — organic as a share of total new pipeline over 4–6 quarters. A 12-month traffic chart is not pipeline share; one anchor point plus a traffic line still cannot answer 'treadmill or moat'.

The compounding-asset view — the thing I underwrote. Ironically the ingredients now exist operator-side (Published mode tracks clicks-since-publish per piece; Content Health tracks decay), but nobody assembled the board sentence: 'pages shipped ≥2 quarters ago are still earning $X/mo at zero incremental spend.' It's an assembly job away and still not designed.

An honest all-in denominator — 'your monthly investment' is retainer-only, while the same kit sells content subscriptions at $600–$2,400/mo as a separate line. If content spend and internal time never enter the denominator, the 4.6× is structurally flattering.
My quarterly two-line push (the only reason I ever open the thing) — the kit designs a destination, not a notification spine; no board-cadence hook is specified anywhere.
Closed-loop CRM reconciliation — value is still modeled (visits × CVR × AOV), not pipeline/closed-won; nothing I can paste into an LP conversation as ROI.
distrustTriggers: A client-facing 94/100 site-health ring in the portal's PROOF column — an invented 0–100 composite the agency controls, my named instant trust kill, back on the first screen after P0 correctly buried it.

The portal's PROOF section leads with impressions/clicks vitals, sparklines, and rank-gain rows ('was #24 · now #12') — rankings-and-impressions-as-results, which all seven of us named as the metric we trust least.

Two coexisting client designs with different value methodologies (portal: 'recovered $4,900 of $14.2k targeted' story vs dashboard: '~$18,400/mo · 4.6× retainer' ledger). If the number in my screenshot depends on which surface the operator opened, that IS the silent-restatement hazard — inconsistent methodology across surfaces is indistinguishable from a moving definition.

Aggressive dollarization as the default register (the digest's own tension #5): modeled arithmetic as the hero figure on nearly every surface, including 'pipeline value at stake $14,200/mo'. Labeled estimates everywhere is honest; estimate-stacked heroes everywhere is a spin gradient — false precision at scale, and it invites my marketing lead to paste a modeled number into the deck as ROI.

The graduation rule is structurally a highlight-reel filter: wins 'graduate' into the client story, losses stay in the workbench. Combined with a self-graded '90-day scorecard: win rate 68%' where the agency assigns its own Win/Early/Flat verdicts, the client narrative has a built-in all-green bias unless dips are forced onto the surface by rule.
'Since your last visit' is a floating comparison window — the cherry-picked-window manipulation my co-panelists flagged; windows must be locked to fixed baselines.
Segment/altitude still appears to be an operator-configured choice — a hand-pickable framing that flatters the number remains a future spin lever until it's derived and locked.
wouldImprove: Resolve the two-client-design fork BEFORE building anything client-facing (the handoff brief's own gate) and lock ONE value methodology, so the number in my quarterly screenshot is the same number regardless of which surface produced it — then version the methodology and show a change-log when it moves.

Add my altitude as a first-class segment rendering of the existing verdict slot: 'Organic CAC $X this quarter, down Y% QoQ, Z% below blended paid; payback N months.' The ROI ledger's 'equivalent ad spend' line is the raw material — reframe it as a CAC comparison instead of a traffic-rental price.

Make the denominator all-in and make it visible: retainer + content subscription + a stated internal-time estimate, itemized in the ledger. Ship the honest denominator BEFORE the first CAC number ever renders, so the first CAC I see never has to be restated downward.

Assemble the compounding-asset view from data the kit already collects: per-piece clicks-since-publish (Published mode) minus decay (Content Health) → one board card: 'work shipped before this quarter is still earning ~$X/mo at zero new spend, N of M pieces holding.' That card is my moat proof and my renewal argument in one.
Add the 4–6 quarter organic-share-of-pipeline slope to the export and the hero — even three points of honest slope beats twelve months of traffic.

Kill the client-facing 94/100 health ring (translate it to the humanized fix list it already sits next to); either provenance-chip the self-graded win-rate scorecard with its scoring rules or drop it.

Ship the quarterly two-line push and wire the one-pager export to whichever client design wins — for the board persona the push and the export ARE the product; the dashboard is just where I go to check the math.
Lock all comparison windows to fixed baselines (engagement start, quarter boundaries); 'since your last visit' can be an additional lens, never the only one.

Put a bad quarter in the design fixtures: show me a red verdict, a down ledger, and an honest 'here's the plan' rendered end-to-end. A trust instrument that has never demonstrated bad news is untested where it matters most.
structuredOutput.verbatim: You've built a genuinely honest estimate machine and a beautiful workbench, but until I can see organic CAC next to paid CAC, a payback figure, and proof that last quarter's pages are still earning this quarter, my one question — treadmill or moat — remains unanswered, so I'll keep funding but I won't argue to expand.

promptHead:
Stay fully in character. Answer ONLY from this persona's perspective and self-interest. Advisory review — no files written, no git.
PERSONA: Junior SEO in their first agency job, second week on the platform.
YOUR JOB ON THIS PLATFORM: Complete assigned tasks (review keywords, prep a brief, run an audit) without breaking anything or asking constantly.
DISTRUST/FRUSTRATION TRIGGERS: Tribal-knowledge

structuredOutput.persona: Junior SEO, second week at the agency — completes assigned tasks (keyword reviews, brief prep, audits) and is terrified of breaking something client-facing or having to ask a senior how to find a screen.
resolved: Keywords 2→1 consolidation ends my daily 'which of three screens has this keyword?' problem — one surface, five lenses, and a Lifecycle kanban that teaches the workflow visually
Cockpit verdict header ('3 things need you today') + three stream tiles tell me what to do without asking anyone
'Replaces Keyword Hub · Page Intelligence' headers and 'was:' badges map the old platform to the new one during my transition
Pipeline cards have exactly one stage-advance CTA (Generate brief → Write draft → …) so I can't pick the wrong next step
AI review gates where factual-accuracy and hallucination checks NEVER auto-pass — protects a junior from publishing a bad claim
Recommendations have real recall ('Waiting on Acme to approve · recall') and undo-on-dismiss — I verified the handlers exist in recs.js
'What Acme sees' side-by-side preview means I can see the client-facing wording before anything ships
Client-declined keywords shown WITH the client's stated reasons — teaches me account preferences without tribal knowledge
Onboarding flow replaces the broken-dashboard cold start, so a new client isn't a trap for a new hire
⌘K palette with fuzzy jump + recents is a real escape hatch once I know it exists
stillMissing: Loading, error, and permission/locked states — the brief mandates them but the prototype shows none, so I can't distinguish 'broken' from 'loading' and will interrupt seniors
Any in-app explanation of the platform's own vocabulary: 'graduate', 'stage into Insights Engine', 'provenance', 'backing move' — no tooltips, glossary, or first-run coach marks
A confirmation step (with recall-window semantics) on 'Send update to {client}' and 'Share with client' — sends are one click + toast, and I can't tell if the client already got an email
Rail entries for Command Center / book Site Health / Action Results — the BOOK zone array is literally empty; logo-click and ⌘K are the only paths
Any role/permission model or reviewer step — nothing stops a week-two junior from sending directly to a client with no senior look
A key legend + undo for the SEO Editor A/R/S/P keyboard review queue — single-key reject with no visible legend is an accident machine
Working Intent/Stage filter chips on Keywords (currently inert) and the unreachable Pipeline matrix mode
Guidance on how defensible the dollar estimates are before I repeat one to a client — methodology exists in some spots but not consistently at point of use
distrustTriggers: Logo-click-only access to book-level views — pure tribal knowledge; I wouldn't find Command Center for weeks
One-click client sends with no confirm dialog and unclear recall semantics (does recall work after the client's email went out?)
Dead controls that look live — Intent/Stage filter chips, History, Export; one inert button makes me doubt every button, and doubt whether my table is secretly filtered
Cockpit 'Fix' buttons that all route to Content Pipeline even for dead-link issues — following the UI lands me in the wrong tool and makes me think I misread the workflow
No visual difference between an error and an empty/loading state — I'll either ship on bad data or ping a senior every time
Purple tinting the whole Optimization nav group while the stated law is 'purple = admin-AI only' — I'd copy the pattern and get corrected
Two coexisting client-facing designs (portal vs tabbed dashboard) — I'd learn one story to tell clients, then be told it's the other one
wouldImprove: Fill the BOOK zone rail with Command Center, Inbox, and book Site Health — don't make the logo a secret button
Put a confirm dialog on every Send/Share that shows the client-view preview and states plainly whether it can still be recalled ('Dana hasn't opened this yet')
Add first-run coach marks and hover '?' definitions for graduate/stage/provenance/backing-move — the GLOSSARY exists in the repo, wire it into the UI
Make every visible control work or delete it — inert filter chips are worse than no chips
Route Fix buttons by issue type (dead links → Links, schema → Schema), not everything → Pipeline
Ship the mandated loading/error/locked states before pilot, with distinct visuals for 'fetching' vs 'failed'
Give the Editor keyboard queue an on-screen key legend and an undo-last-action, so a stray 'R' isn't a silent rejection
Add a junior/reviewer role where my client sends queue for a senior's one-click approval instead of firing immediately
structuredOutput.verbatim: Honestly, the Keywords merge and the one-button pipeline cards would make my second week so much easier — I just need the send buttons to double-check me and the important screens to actually be in the sidebar, because I'm not going to guess that the logo is a door.

promptHead:
Stay fully in character. Answer ONLY from this persona's perspective and self-interest. Advisory review — no files written, no git.
PERSONA: 47-year-old owner-dentist, single-location family practice. Chairside 8-5, not a marketer. Pays a retainer to fill empty chairs and wants proof in plain English — calls, bookings, reviews, dollars — matching what the front desk actually saw.
DISTRUST TRIGGERS

Stay fully in character. Answer ONLY from this persona's perspective and self-interest. Advisory review — no files written, no git.
PERSONA: Solo founder-operator of hmpsn.studio itself. Runs every client workspace personally, AI-assisted, a few hours per week per client. The admin side of this platform is your daily cockpit.
YOUR JOB ON THIS PLATFORM: Get through all workspaces fast: triage what

structuredOutput.persona: Solo founder-operator of hmpsn.studio — I personally run every client workspace a few hours a week, AI-assisted; the admin side is my daily cockpit and context-switching cost is everything.
resolved: The Command Center is finally MY home: one cross-client work queue organized by the three questions I actually ask each morning (what to fix, what to send, what grows revenue) — it replaces my current ritual of clicking into every workspace to find what needs me. That alone pays for the redesign.

The per-client Cockpit answers 'what needs me today for THIS client' in one screen — verdict sentence, work queue with impact figures, portal replies, content-in-flight — instead of today's Home + Requests + Pipeline + Analytics tab-hop.
Keywords 2→1 genuinely consolidates my Keyword Hub + Page Intelligence split into one table with five lenses and a detail drawer; that's a real click-count reduction on a surface I live in.
The SEO Editor keyboard review queue (A/R/S/P, arrow keys) and sticky bulk bar are built for volume clearing — that's exactly how I want to burn down approval stacks.
The Recommendations dual pane (operator jargon left, 'What Acme sees' right, same rec) kills my most tedious step: re-authoring internal findings into client language before every send.
The send boundary + graduation rule means I always know what's client-visible without double-checking — technical noise stays in my workbench, only provable stories reach the client.
Promotable requests closing the loop (portal reply → cockpit → strategy signal → backing move) is the wiring today's platform half-has; here it's a first-class spine.
Cold-start onboarding replacing a broken empty dashboard for new clients — today I explain the empty states to myself; this turns setup into the surface.
⌘K palette with fuzzy workspace jump and persisted recents keeps my fastest switch path alive.

Pipeline's Published mode as a results read-back with 'Add to Insights' graduation, and Content Health's queue-refresh decay loop — those are the two loops I currently run manually across three screens.
stillMissing: The BOOK zone is EMPTY (nav.js line 14: const book = [];) — Command Center, book Site Health, and Action Results, the surfaces my whole job runs on, are reachable only via logo-click or ⌘K. My most-used destination has no nav entry.

Zero loading, error, or degraded-connection states in the prototype. Half my real operating days are 'GSC token expired' or 'SEMRush quota hit'. An empty work queue that is actually a failed fetch means I skip a client who needed me — and nothing here distinguishes the two.

Verdict sentences carry no data-freshness or provenance of their own. Dollar figures get estimate/measured/actual chips, but the auto-generated 'Acme is on track' headline — the thing I'd act on first — shows nothing about how stale its inputs are.

Recommendations is a 4-card flat list with no filters, search, aging sort, or volume handling. Across a full book with audits generating recs, that queue is 50+ items in week one; the money surface is the thinnest view in the kit.
Inbox/Requests is the smallest core module (~9KB) despite being the entire two-way client boundary — no bulk actions, no per-client grouping, no snooze/triage. It's where my mornings start.
No fast book-walk mechanic: no next/prev client cycling, no cockpit-by-cockpit sweep. ⌘K is good for targeted jumps but my job is a serial sweep of 10+ workspaces.

The consolidation story is internally inconsistent — Site Audit badged 3→1 and documented as absorbing Performance and Links, yet both still ship as separate rail items. I need to learn ONE map, not a claimed one and a shipped one.
No visible background-job/notification equivalent to today's NotificationBell — I run crawls, audits, and generations all day and need job status in the chrome, not per-surface.

Pipeline's Matrix bulk-planning mode is built but has no button to reach it, and the Keywords Intent/Stage filter chips are inert — in a 'prototype is the spec' regime, those are spec holes on my highest-volume surfaces.

The two client-facing designs (portal microsite vs tabbed dashboard) are unresolved and that decision gates everything client-side — until I make that call the whole 'send boundary' half of my loop is provisional.
distrustTriggers: Logo-click as the only rail path to the Command Center — adding a hidden, unlabeled hop to the loop I run twenty times a day is exactly the 'redesign adds clicks' failure mode.
Cockpit 'Fix' buttons ALL route to the Content Pipeline, including dead-link fixes that belong in Links — one button that lands me in the wrong room and I stop trusting every button.

Mint-for-teal palette swap plus a fully regrouped rail breaks years of muscle memory at once, and the purple tint on the whole Optimization nav group violates my own purple-is-admin-AI-only law inside the same kit.
Inert chrome shipping as designed (filter chips with no handlers, History, Auto-share rules, calendar arrows) — dead controls teach me to distrust live ones.
Silently switching me to a DIFFERENT client when I navigate a scoped view on a cold-start client — a silent client-context swap is a mis-send incident waiting to happen.

Auto-generated verdict prose and headline dollarization (visits × CVR × AOV as the hero everywhere, including client-facing) with no per-client control — if a client challenges a number I didn't author and can't defend, the whole trust spine backfires on me.
No empty-vs-error distinction anywhere — a queue that says 'nothing needs you' over a broken data connection is the single most dangerous screen this design could ship.
wouldImprove: Fill the BOOK rail zone it already declares: Command Center, Site Health, Action Results as permanent top-of-rail items with badges — my home should never be a logo Easter egg.
Give me a book-sweep mechanic: ⌘]/⌘[ next/previous client cycling and a j/k walk through cockpits, so my morning is a keyboard pass, not thirty clicks.
Route every cockpit Fix button to the surface that actually fixes that issue type — links to Links, alt text to Assets, schema to Schema — never a blanket redirect to Pipeline.

Put a connection/freshness strip on every verdict header ('GSC 2h ago · GA4 stale 3d — verdict may be stale') and make the verdict inherit its worst input's provenance chip, same ladder as the dollars.
Make empty-vs-error a design-system law: every empty queue must state data freshness and degrade loudly when a source is down, with a reconnect CTA inline.
Beef up Recommendations to match its job: status filters, client filter, aging sort ('waiting on Acme 9d'), bulk triage — it will hold 50+ items, design for that day one.
Resolve the 3→1 Site Audit claim before build — either Performance and Links truly fold in or the badge goes; I refuse to learn a nav map that lies about itself.

Wire a global background-job bell into the bottom utility bar next to Inbox — crawls, audits, and generations need one status home across the whole book.
